text.title: Contract-First Development Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Contract-First Development application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Write the OpenAPI spec before any server code; no implementation file is created until the spec passes spectral lint and human review.
rule.rationale: Code-first inverts the source of truth and lets schemas drift between server, client, and docs. Spec-first keeps every generated artefact derivable from one file.

rule.statement: Define all request/response schemas using $ref to components/schemas; never inline schema definitions in path operations.
rule.rationale: Inlined schemas duplicate types across paths; one definition silently diverges from the others. $ref is the only way generators can name and reuse types reliably.

rule.statement: Run spectral lint on every PR that touches openapi.yaml; treat operation-operationId and oas3-schema rules as errors, not warnings.
rule.rationale: operationId is the codegen handle; without it, generated client methods get auto-named and break across spec edits. Errors must block merge.

rule.statement: Run oasdiff breaking against the main branch spec on every PR; block merge on any ERR-level breaking change unless the commit body contains a `BREAKING-CHANGE:` token with justification.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes shipped silently cascade into client crashes. The override token forces the author to consciously declare and document the break.

rule.statement: Hand-editing generated server stubs or client SDKs is forbidden; modifications flow only through spec changes followed by regeneration.
rule.rationale: Hand-edits to generated code are silently overwritten on the next regeneration. The change is lost; the regression appears unrelated.

rule.statement: Bump openapi.info.version following semver: PATCH for editorial fixes, MINOR for additive endpoints/fields, MAJOR for any breaking change.
rule.rationale: Without semver discipline, consumers cannot reason about upgrade safety; every release becomes a manual diff hunt.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
